observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to (i) submit the lacking documents as enumerated, in compliance with the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (ii) sternly observe the circumstances mentioned in the procurement law prior to employing the alternative modes of procurement.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations and the LCE agreed to (i) direct the Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (ii) give preferential attention in registering the seven parcels of land under the name of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), agreed to (i) cause the formal constitution of clear guidelines on telephone expenses by setting the parameters as to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the reasonable limit of the monthly entitlement, taking into consideration the value for money packages offered by telecommunication companies vis-à-vis the actual need to carry out official tasks; and (ii) ensure the inclusion in the policy guidelines of a provision guaranteeing the Municipality’s ownership over the mobile phone units in the post-paid plans.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (i) MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-02; and (ii) concerned officials to be informed of the reportorial and monitoring requirement as mentioned in COA Circular No. 2014-002 and keep track of the remaining undistributed/unconsummated goods for them to be properly accounted, monitored and distributed to avoid loss and wastage.

We recommended that the Management continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented.
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) BAC and its Secretariat to fully comply with the provision of the Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184 on the (i) posting of Contracts and NTPs; (ii) inviting observers during bid evaluations and post-qualification activities; and (iii) sending of notifications of bidding results to losing bidders; (b) BAC and TWG to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion; and (c) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enhance the GAD AR by completely filling out the needed qualitative aspect of the activities implemented including data on sex-aggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer to continue their collaborative efforts in pursuing future LGSF-funded programs in accordance with the guidelines set by the DILG, as the case may be; (b) BAC and its Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents as enumerated and warrant the completeness of supporting documents that need to be prepared/submitted in the procurement stages of project in accordance with the provisions of DILG MC 2020-088 and R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary documents are attached to the DVs are present before making the payment/s.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain separate special accounts and set up subsidiary ledgers for each of the LEEs in the GF; and (ii) prepare the corresponding schedules to support the financial statements for the LEEs, which will serve as tools the Management can use in evaluating the viability of the LEEs operated by the Municipality.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We recommended and the Management agreed continue to (i) withhold and remit the loan amortization and mandatory contributions to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; and (ii) exert extra effort in establishing the accuracy of the balances in the inter-agency payables.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to prepare, update and submit the required LDRRMF Investment Plan annually; and review the DRRM projects and activities that were not implemented in CY 2020 and program their execution, if still warranted by the present circumstances or program new PPAs that may effectively address the demands of the current situation so that calamity-issues sought to be addressed by the initiatives are given the necessary solutions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to - utilize the cash donations for the intended purpose; and instruct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies to foster compliance to existing rules and promote transparency and accountability in the administration of donations intended to augment the needs of the constituents during the lockdown/quarantine period.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented.

We recommended and Management agreed that all concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and comply with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality continue to withhold and remit the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHIC.
We recommended and Management agreed to exert efforts to enforce settlement of all suspensions as required under COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct Municipal Accountant to:

exert efforts to locate and trace necessary records and pertinent documents to establish validity and existence of the Due from LGUs account; and

undertake necessary procedures in derecognizing the accounts from the books of the Municipality in accordance with the guidelines and procedures set by COA Circular 2016-005 for accounts qualified as dormant.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

require the Bids and Awards Committee to procure the common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) from Procurement Service of the DBM; and

ensure that procurement of CSEs other than the PS depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in the PS depot should be supported by a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS).
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the Municipality still has procurement of common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) other than DBM-Procurement Service.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert efforts to locate and trace necessary records and pertinent documents to establish validity and existence of the Due from LGUs account.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated the audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the General Services Officer to (i) conduct complete physical count of all the PPE and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and (ii) maintain property records such as Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation with accounting records could be made; and
give preferential attention on registering the seven parcels of land in favor and under the name of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned agency officials and employees to expedite the implementation of the projects and programs funded by the National Government Agencies so that these projects could immediately serve their purposes for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended and the -
Management agreed to continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented;
Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to submit the lacking documents; and
Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities.

We recommended and the -
Local Chief Executive agreed to (i) direct the MDRRMO to ensure the compliance of the auditee with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness; and (ii) require the responsible officers to submit appropriate documents supporting the distribution of the goods; and
Accountant agreed to draft a JEV to reclassify the purchased equipment from expense account to appropriate asset account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct (i) all concerned personnel to expedite the review and submission of documents pertaining to their transactions so as not to cause delays and (ii) Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (i) make a representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the updating of the Local Revenue Code; and (ii) direct the Municipal Treasurer to improve the collection strategies for local taxes.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to (i) work on the proper identification of its members and consider adopting the functions, roles and responsibilities of GFPS, LCE, Executive Committee, TWG and Secretariat in full compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC 2016-01 and (ii) ensure that the GAD Accomplishment Report is properly supported with all the required reports.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality continue to withhold and remit the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHIC.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

require the concerned officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances by submitting the required report and settlement documents to the Accounting Office and refund the unexpended balance to the Treasury Office, if there is any, and 

ensure that rules and regulations on reporting cash advances are strictly observed by all accountable officers within the prescribed period.

We recommended and management agreed to give preferential attention on registering the seven parcels of land in favor and under the name of the Municipality. 

We also reiterated the audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the General Services Officer to: 

conduct complete physical count of all the PPE and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and

maintain property records such as Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation with accounting records could be made.

We reiterated our audit recommendations and management agreed to require the Municipal LSB to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF).

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive as Chairperson of the Municipal Local School Board agreed to stop the practice of utilizing the SEF for expenses not related to or expressly provided in Sections 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017.

We reiterated our audit recommendations and management agreed to:

direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP and ensure the compliance of the auditee with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness; and

consider filling-up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions and employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs and activities. 

We also recommended and management agreed to:

observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund in order to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents, before, during or after disaster/calamities; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe proper controls in recording the purchases of relief goods.
observations.reason: Some findings were still observed and reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

continuously communicate with EMB and NSWMC to fast-track and finally obtain approval of the 10-year SWM plan;

make improvements and install the needed mechanisms and facilities to render the MRFs operational; and 
instruct the Barangay officials and members of the MSWM Board to conduct evaluation of the MRFs in the Municipality and institute measures to improve their operations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period to facilitate timely audit of the Municipality’s GAD activities and accomplishments.

We reiterated our audit recommendation and management agreed to:

strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and 

observe the prescribed period for submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
We recommended and management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource and Management Officer to exert conscionable effort in gathering all pertinent documents in order to analyze the subject accounts and settle the related variances.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the General Services Officer to:

conduct complete physical count of all the PPE of the Municipality; 

complete the preparation of RPCPPE and submit copy to the Audit Team; and

maintain property records such as Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation with accounting records will be made.

We reiterated our recommendation and Municipal Treasurer agreed to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for recording the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.

We recommended and Management agreed to exercise judicious planning on their identified priority projects/programs/activities prior to allocating resources thereto and integrating it in the AIP; maximize the use of funds and implement fully the priority programs and projects; and ensure that utilization of 20 percent development fund is in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: Non-implementation of some PPAs was still observed and noted in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to (i) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (ii) ensure that required financial reports and documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Similar observation was noted and reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

Human Resource Office raise immediately the detected discrepancies in the billing or statement of account from GSIS, PHIC and Pag-IBIG; and

Accounting Office regularly reconcile its records pertaining to GSIS, PHIC and Pag-IBIG and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct Municipal Accountant to:
exert efforts to locate and trace necessary records and pertinent documents to establish validity and existence of the Due from LGUs account; and
undertake necessary procedures in derecognizing the accounts from the books of the Municipality in accordance with the guidelines and procedures set by COA Circular 2016-005 for accounts qualified as dormant.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (i) require the concerned officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances by submitting the required report and settlement documents to the Accounting Office and refund the unexpended balance to the Treasury Office, if there is any, and (ii) ensure that rules and regulations on reporting cash advances are strictly observed by all accountable officers within the prescribed period.

We recommended and management agreed to give preferential attention on registering the seven parcels of land in favor and under the name of the Municipality.
We also reiterated the audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the General Services Officer to (a) conduct complete physical count of all the PPE and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and (b) maintain property records such as Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation with accounting records could be made.

We reiterated our audit recommendations and management agreed to require the Municipal LSB to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF).
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive as Chairperson of the Municipal Local School Board agreed to stop the practice of utilizing the SEF for expenses not related to or expressly provided in Sections 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017.

We reiterated our audit recommendations and management agreed to:
direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP and ensure the compliance of the auditee with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness; and
consider filling-up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions and employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs and activities.
We also recommended and management agreed to:
observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund in order to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents, before, during or after disaster/calamities; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe proper controls in recording the purchases of relief goods.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
continuously communicate with EMB and NSWMC to fast-track and finally obtain approval of the 10-year SWM plan;
make improvements and install the needed mechanisms and facilities to render the MRFs operational; and
instruct the Barangay officials and members of the MSWM Board to conduct evaluation of the MRFs in the Municipality and institute measures to improve their operations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period to facilitate timely audit of the Municipality’s GAD activities and accomplishments.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Bids and Awards Committee to procure the common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) from Procurement Service of the DBM and ensure that procurement of CSEs other than the PS depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in the PS depot should be supported by a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS).

We reiterated our recommendations and management agreed to (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the prescribed period for submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
We recommended and management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource and Management Officer to exert conscionable effort in gathering all pertinent documents in order to analyze the subject accounts and settle the related variances.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider the negotiation with government bank to enroll in the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system to lessen the risks of loss, strengthen controls in handling cash for payroll, and improve efficiency in treasury operations.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) stop the practice of paying fixed amount for communication without the submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees; and (ii) consider the availment of monthly contract subscriptions with telephone companies offering discounts or services promotions.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and Advisory No. 2016-02 dated November 25, 2016 of the Interim National Peace and Order Council Secretariat of DILG relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, and use of the confidential funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

require the LSB to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and

require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report of SEF Utilization quarterly and annually using the prescribed form for submission to appropriate offices.
observations.reason: Non-compliance in the SEF Budget preparation and approval is still observable and reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

consider the timelines of project and improve its monitoring functions or supervision for projects being implemented to avoid incurrence of delays;

direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities; and 

ensure that the Construction Safety and Health Program is approved by DOLE wherein its cost is embedded in the project’s cost and ultimately in the contract or agreement between the LGU and the contractor.
observations.reason: Delays in implementation were still observed and noted in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that (i) the required LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report of Utilization be prepared and submitted on a regular basis; and (ii) the LDRRMC prioritize the implementation of projects, activities and programs on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times. 

We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality (i) refrain from charging against LDRRMF expenses which are not in accordance with the purposes declared on the above-cited law; (ii) consider filling-up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions and employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs; and (iii) comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness.
observations.reason: Non-compliance with some of audit recommendations were reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to exert best effort to (i) complete the necessary revisions or updating and resubmit the revised 10-year plan to EMB and NSWMC satisfying all the requirements and comments; and (ii) cause the construction of a functional and operational central MRF.
observations.reason: The Municipality already constructed the central MRF, however, there were some observations noted and included in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to:

allot time and resources for the full establishment or completion of the GAD database;

ensure the implementation of the entire GAD PPAs so that desired results are achieved, and the GAD issues are properly addressed; and

submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the time prescribed.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of GPB and GAD AR was still observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Acting Human Resource and Management Officer to (i) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balance ₱520,352.43; and (ii) remit all the withheld taxes and premiums intact and within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund; 

observe the provisions of the Market Code on the execution of contracts between the Municipality and market stallholders; and

enforce the “one person, one business” policy in the concept of fairness and transparency.
observations.reason: Non-compliance to the Market Code is still observable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for recording the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) prioritize the implementation of projects, activities and programs on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times.
observations.reason: Similar finding was observed and discussed in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management give preferential attention on the processing of documents needed for the issuance by the Registry of Deeds of the Transfer Certificate of Title of the mentioned lots in the Municipality’s name pursuant to Section 39(2) of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive coordinate the efforts of public and private sectors to ensure the full implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003 and work to accelerate the construction and proper utilization of the MRF of the Municipality and hasten the implementation of other solid waste management projects or programs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain reports/property records such as RPCPPE and Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation of accounting records with physical count will be made. Likewise, conduct physical count on all the properties of the Municipality with the Auditor as witness.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group:

Facilitate the full establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU;

Formulate and pass an ordinance for the establishment of GAD code to support the LGU’s efforts to promote gender equality; and

Assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that should be in place.

We recommended that the management continue to implement projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% development fund on a timely basis to guarantee that the Municipality’s constituents fully enjoy the benefits of all programmed projects.
observations.reason: Unfavorable finding was observed and discussed in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer remit all withheld taxes intact and on time.
observations.reason: Unfavorable finding was observed and discussed in this report.

observations.recommendation:
1.5	We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the General Services Officer to:
conduct complete physical count of all the PPE of the Municipality;
complete the preparation of RPCPPE and submit copy to the Audit Team; and
maintain property records such as Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation with accounting records will be made.

We reiterated our recommendation and Municipal Treasurer agreed to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for recording the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.

3.9	We recommended and Management agreed to consider the negotiation with government bank to enroll in the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system to lessen the risks of loss, strengthen controls in handling cash for payroll, and improve efficiency in treasury operations.

4.12 	We recommended and Management agreed to (i) stop the practice of paying fixed amount for communication without the submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees; and (ii) consider the availment of monthly contract subscriptions with telephone companies offering discounts or services promotions.

5.14	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and Advisory No. 2016-02 dated November 25, 2016 of the Interim National Peace and Order Council Secretariat of DILG relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, and use of the confidential funds.

6.12	We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the LSB to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report of SEF Utilization quarterly and annually using the prescribed form for submission to appropriate offices.

7.10	We recommended and Management agreed to exercise judicious planning on their identified priority projects/programs/activities prior to allocating resources thereto and integrating it in the AIP; maximize the use of funds and implement fully the priority programs and projects; and ensure that utilization of 20 percent development fund is in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

8.13	We recommended and Management agreed to:
consider the timelines of project and improve its monitoring functions or supervision for projects being implemented to avoid incurrence of delays;
direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities; and
ensure that the Construction Safety and Health Program is approved by DOLE wherein its cost is embedded in the project’s cost and ultimately in the contract or agreement between the LGU and the contractor.

9.8	We recommended and Management agreed that (i) the required LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report of Utilization be prepared and submitted on a regular basis; and (ii) the LDRRMC prioritize the implementation of projects, activities and programs on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times.

9.9	We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality (i) refrain from charging against LDRRMF expenses which are not in accordance with the purposes declared on the above-cited law; (ii) consider filling-up the vacant MDRMM Staff/Officer plantilla positions and employ able/competent individuals who will assist the Head of the MDRRMO in carrying out disaster risk management programs; and (iii) comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness.

10.12	 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to exert best effort to (i) complete the necessary revisions or updating and resubmit the revised 10-year plan to EMB and NSWMC satisfying all the requirements and comments; and (ii) cause the construction of a functional and operational central MRF.

11.3	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to:
allot time and resources for the full establishment or completion of the GAD database;
ensure the implementation of the entire GAD PPAs so that desired results are achieved and the GAD issues are properly addressed; and
submit copies of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the time prescribed.

12.6	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to (i) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (ii) ensure that required financial reports and documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

13.8	We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Human Resource Office raise immediately the detected discrepancies in the billing or statement of account from GSIS, PHIC and Pag-IBIG; and
Accounting Office regularly reconcile its records pertaining to GSIS, PHIC and Pag-IBIG and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

14.5	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Acting Human Resource and Management Officer to (i) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balance ₱520,352.43; and (ii) remit all the withheld taxes and premiums intact and within the prescribed period.
15.2	We recommended and Management agreed to continue to exert efforts to enforce settlement of all suspensions and disallowances as required under COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

16.4	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund;
observe the provisions of the Market Code on the execution of contracts between the Municipality and market stallholders; and
enforce the “one person, one business” policy in the concept of fairness and transparency.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate the Municipality’s own implementing guidelines, policies and procedures regarding AICS, in conformity with the provisions set forth by the DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014, as basis in the granting of financial or in-kind assistances to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated under Section 4.2 of DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014 to establish the validity of the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in giving financial assistance (a) strictly adhere to the provisions set forth under DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014; and (b) use the assessment report of the MSWDO in determining the amount to be given to beneficiaries.

We recommended that the municipal officials who attended the said seminar refund the cash advances for this undertaking for want of legal basis. As of report writing, only ₱33,440.00 was refunded leaving a balance of ₱8,480.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Concerned Offices to furnish copies of POs to the Audit Team within five days after issuance and copies of Inspection and Acceptance or its equivalent within 24 hours after their receipt of goods; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee to consolidate all procurement requests (PPMPs) and prepare the APP.

We recommended that the General Services Officer transfer immediately the accountability of the above mentioned equipment to employees of the Municipality with a permanent status by issuing new Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment (AREs) to ensure the proper use and care of all their properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer its deposits with the Philippine Postal Savings Bank to the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) in consonance with DOF Department Circular No. 001-2015 in order to protect funds from possible loss.

We recommended that the Local School Board (a) ensure that the budgeted amount for each year on SEF projects is implemented/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents; and (b) request for inputs from the DepEd officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund which includes non-allocation of funds for administrative expenses and to avoid the increase of unutilized savings.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the implementing office and personnel concerned to expedite the implementation and accomplishment of the projects funded by the National Government Agencies so that these projects could immediately serve their purposes to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough resources to support their daily needs until the next payday.
observations.reason: There were still two permanent employees with NTHP below ₱3,500.00 as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local SB members submit documents to support actual performance of work to determine that their claim for RATA is in conformity with the aforementioned rules and regulations.

1.8 	We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate the Municipality’s own implementing guidelines, policies and procedures regarding AICS, in conformity with the provisions set forth by the DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014, as basis in the granting of financial or in-kind assistances to its constituents.

2.4 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated under Section 4.2 of DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014 to establish the validity of the claims.

3.4 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in giving financial assistance (a) strictly adhere to the provisions set forth under DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014; and (b) use the assessment report of the MSWDO in determining the amount to be given to beneficiaries.

4.6 	Finding that the seminar on “Best Practices and Remedies to Avoid COA Disallowances” was not authorized by the Department of the Interior and Local Government or any other government agency, it was recommended that the municipal officials who attended the said seminar refund the cash advances for this undertaking for want of legal basis. As of report writing, only P 33,440.00 was refunded leaving a balance of P8,480.00.

5.4 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Concerned Offices to furnish copies of POs to the Audit Team within five days after issuance and copies of Inspection and Acceptance or its equivalent within 24 hours after their receipt of goods; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee to consolidate all procurement requests (PPMPs) and prepare the APP.

6.7 	We recommended that the General Services Officer transfer immediately the accountability of the above mentioned equipment to employees of the Municipality with a permanent status by issuing new Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment (AREs) to ensure the proper use and care of all their properties.

7.6 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer its deposits with the Philippine Postal Savings Bank to the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) in consonance with DOF Department Circular No. 001-2015 in order to protect funds from possible loss.

8.9 	We recommended that the Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for recording the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.

9.14 	We recommended that the Local School Board (a) ensure that the budgeted amount for each year on SEF projects is implemented/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents; and (b) request for inputs from the DepEd officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund which includes non-allocation of funds for administrative expenses and to avoid the increase of unutilized savings.

10.5 	We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) prioritize the implementation of projects, activities and programs on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times.

11.9 	We recommended that management give preferential attention on the processing of documents needed for the issuance by the Registry of Deeds of the Transfer Certificate of Title of the mentioned lots in the Municipality’s name pursuant to Section 39(2) of P.D. No. 1445.

12.8 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the implementing office and personnel concerned to expedite the implementation and accomplishment of the projects funded by the National Government Agencies so that these projects could immediately serve their purposes to the intended beneficiaries.

13.20 	We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive coordinate the efforts of public and private sectors to ensure the full implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003 and work to accelerate the construction and proper utilization of the MRF of the Municipality and hasten the implementation of other solid waste management projects or programs.

14.6 	We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain reports/property records such as RPCPPE and Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed of so that reconciliation of accounting records with physical count will be made. Likewise, conduct physical count on all the properties of the Municipality with the Auditor as witness.

16.3 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough resources to support their daily needs until the next payday.

17.9 	We reiterated our recommendation that the Local SB members submit documents to support actual performance of work to determine that their claim for RATA is in conformity with the aforementioned rules and regulations.

18.4 	We recommended that the management continue to implement projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% development fund on a timely basis to guarantee that the Municipality’s constituents fully enjoy the benefits of all programmed projects.
19.2 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer remit all withheld taxes intact and on time.
